cot_prefix: Okay, let's check the passage to see what the American scientists' search group did. The relevant part says: "In 1975, however, some American scientists formed a search group. They used an underwater camera. It took pictures every seventy seconds. Some of the pictures seemed to show a red-brown creature. Its body was about four meters long and had a very ugly head on the end of a four meter neck. Many people then began to believe in the monster. But even today we can not be certain." So the scientists formed a group in 1975, used an underwater camera, and some of the pictures they took seemed to show a monster-like creature. This made many people believe in the monster, but there's still no certainty. Now, looking at the options: (A) found the monster itself - No, they didn't find the monster, just some pictures that seemed to show it. (B) found a huge cave under water - The cave was found earlier, in the part before 1975. (C) believed that there wasn't any monster at all - The
